frameworks.name: ingredients of intelligence
frameworks.one_line: Catalogue of core cognitive building blocks needed for human-like machine intelligence.

frameworks.name: intuitive physics / intuitive psychology
frameworks.one_line: Start-up software priors for object dynamics and agent goals invoked as ingredients.

frameworks.name: compositional / causal model-building
frameworks.one_line: Symbolic, structured generative models supporting rapid learning from few examples.

frameworks.name: sub-symbolic connectionism
frameworks.one_line: Statistical pattern learners contrasted with structured symbolic accounts.

core_question: How should the proposed key ingredients of human-like intelligence be reconciled with classic debates over their origin, coherence, and representational format?
key_assumptions: Human-like learning requires more than current statistical pattern recognition
Classic AI/cognitive-science debates can be moved past rather than re-fought
A short set of identifiable ingredients can be enumerated and engineered
manipulations: Survey commentary responses across three contrast axes: nature vs nurture, coherent theories vs theory fragments, symbolic vs sub-symbolic
key_findings: Commentators broadly endorsed the ingredients list but disagreed on origin and structure of those ingredients
Disagreements cluster along three classical axes: nature/nurture, coherent theories vs theory fragments, symbolic vs sub-symbolic
Additional ingredients proposed by commentators are judged important for the long run
interpretations: Progress requires moving beyond the three classical debates rather than taking sides within them
Engineering human-like intelligence is a long-run program requiring integration of further ingredients and attention to ethical concerns
limitations_noted: The proposed ingredients set is acknowledged as incomplete
